The approach reveals that structured reasoning guidance unlocks latent capabilities in smaller language models, enabling practical AI education deployment.
content:
arXiv:2603.20650v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Deploying high-fidelity AI tutors in schools is often blocked by the Resource Curse -- the need for expensive cloud GPUs and massive data engineering. In this practitioner report, we present a replicable Standard Operating Procedure that breaks this barrier. Using a Vision-Language Model data cleaning strategy and a novel Shadow-RAG architecture, we localized a graduate-level Applied Mathematics tutor using only 3 person-days of non-expert labor and open-weights 32B models deployable on a single consumer-grade GPU. Our pilot study on a full graduate-level final exam reveals a striking emergence phenomenon: while both zero-shot baselines and standard retrieval stagnate around 50-60% accuracy across model generations, the Shadow Agent, which provides structured reasoning guidance, triggers a massive capability surge in newer 32B models, boosting performance from 74% (Naive RAG) to mastery level (90%). In contrast, older models see only modest gains (~10%). This suggests that such guidance is the key to unlocking the latent power of modern small language models. This work offers a cost-effective, scientifically grounded blueprint for ubiquitous AI education.

zh.title: 从50%到精通：3天内通过Shadow-RAG本地化研究生级AI导师的低资源标准程序
zh.summary: 研究人员演示了一种成本效益高的方法，使用新颖的Shadow-RAG架构在消费级GPU上部署复杂的AI导师，在研究生级考试中达到90%的准确率。该方法表明结构化推理指导释放了较小语言模型中的潜在能力，实现了实际的AI教育部署。

fr.title: De 50% à la Maîtrise en 3 Jours : Procédure Opérationnelle Standard à Faibles Ressources pour Localiser les Tuteurs IA de Niveau Doctorat via Shadow-RAG
fr.summary: Les chercheurs démontrent une méthode rentable pour déployer des tuteurs IA sophistiqués sur des GPU grand public en utilisant une architecture Shadow-RAG novatrice, atteignant une précision de 90% sur les examens de niveau doctorat. L'approche révèle que les conseils de raisonnement structuré libèrent les capacités latentes dans les modèles de langage plus petits, permettant un déploiement pratique d'éducation IA.

de.title: Von 50% zur Meisterschaft in 3 Tagen: Eine Low-Resource-SOP zur Lokalisierung von Graduate-Level-KI-Tutoren über Shadow-RAG
de.summary: Forscher demonstrieren eine kostengünstige Methode zum Einsatz ausgefeilter KI-Tutoren auf Consumer-Grade-GPUs unter Verwendung einer neuartigen Shadow-RAG-Architektur, wobei auf Graduate-Level-Prüfungen 90% Genauigkeit erreicht wird. Der Ansatz zeigt, dass strukturierte Reasoning-Anleitung verborgene Fähigkeiten in kleineren Sprachmodellen freisetzt und eine praktische KI-Bildungsbereitstellung ermöglicht.

es.title: De 50% a Dominio en 3 Días: Un Procedimiento Operativo Estándar de Bajo Recurso para Localizar Tutores de IA de Nivel de Posgrado vía Shadow-RAG
es.summary: Los investigadores demuestran un método rentable para desplegar tutores de IA sofisticados en GPUs de consumidor utilizando una arquitectura Shadow-RAG novedosa, logrando una precisión del 90% en exámenes de nivel de posgrado. El enfoque revela que la orientación de razonamiento estructurado desbloquea capacidades latentes en modelos de lenguaje más pequeños, permitiendo un despliegue práctico de educación con IA.

title: Attention in Space: Functional Roles of VLM Heads for Spatial Reasoning
summary: The research investigates how specific attention heads in vision-language models contribute to spatial reasoning through mechanistic interpretability, finding that spatially-specialized heads are sparse and critical. The study identifies methods to activate latent spatial heads to improve multimodal models' spatial understanding.
content:
arXiv:2603.20662v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Despite remarkable advances in large Vision-Language Models (VLMs), spatial reasoning remains a persistent challenge. In this work, we investigate how attention heads within VLMs contribute to spatial reasoning by analyzing their functional roles through a mechanistic interpretability lens. We introduce CogVSR, a dataset that decomposes complex spatial reasoning questions into step-by-step subquestions designed to simulate human-like reasoning via a chain-of-thought paradigm, with each subquestion linked to specific cognitive functions such as spatial perception or relational reasoning. Building on CogVSR, we develop a probing framework to identify and characterize attention heads specialized for these functions. Our analysis across diverse VLM families reveals that these functional heads are universally sparse, vary in number and distribution across functions. Notably, spatially specialized heads are fewer than those for other cognitive functions, highlighting their scarcity. We propose methods to activate latent spatial heads, improving spatial understanding. Intervention experiments further demonstrate their critical role in spatial reasoning: removing functional heads leads to performance degradation, while emphasizing them enhances accuracy. This study provides new interpretability driven insights into how VLMs attend to space and paves the way for enhancing complex spatial reasoning in multimodal models.

zh.title: 空间中的注意力：VLM注意力头对空间推理的功能角色
zh.summary: 该研究通过机制可解释性研究了视觉-语言模型中特定注意力头对空间推理的贡献，发现空间专化的注意力头稀疏而关键。该研究确定了激活潜在空间注意力头的方法，以改进多模态模型的空间理解。

fr.title: L'Attention dans l'Espace : Rôles Fonctionnels des Têtes VLM pour le Raisonnement Spatial
fr.summary: La recherche enquête sur la façon dont les têtes d'attention spécifiques dans les modèles de vision et de langage contribuent au raisonnement spatial par le biais de l'interprétabilité mécaniste, découvrant que les têtes spécialisées spatialement sont clairsemées et critiques. L'étude identifie les méthodes pour activer les têtes spatiales latentes afin d'améliorer la compréhension spatiale des modèles multimodaux.

de.title: Aufmerksamkeit im Raum: Funktionale Rollen von VLM-Köpfen für räumliches Denken
de.summary: Die Forschung untersucht, wie spezifische Aufmerksamkeitsköpfe in Visions-Sprache-Modellen durch mechanistische Interpretierbarkeit zum räumlichen Denken beitragen, und stellt fest, dass räumlich spezialisierte Köpfe spärlich und entscheidend sind. Die Studie identifiziert Methoden zur Aktivierung latenter räumlicher Köpfe, um das räumliche Verständnis multimodaler Modelle zu verbessern.

es.title: Atención en el Espacio: Roles Funcionales de las Cabezas VLM para el Razonamiento Espacial
es.summary: La investigación investiga cómo las cabezas de atención específicas en modelos de visión y lenguaje contribuyen al razonamiento espacial a través de la interpretabilidad mecanicista, descubriendo que las cabezas especializadas espacialmente son escasas y críticas. El estudio identifica métodos para activar cabezas espaciales latentes con el fin de mejorar la comprensión espacial de los modelos multimodales.

title: Towards Intelligent Geospatial Data Discovery: a knowledge graph-driven multi-agent framework powered by large language models
summary: This framework uses knowledge graphs and multi-agent collaboration to improve geospatial data discovery with semantic understanding and intent-awareness, outperforming traditional keyword-based systems. It bridges heterogeneous metadata standards to make distributed geospatial data more discoverable and semantically accessible.
content:
arXiv:2603.20670v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rapid growth in the volume, variety, and velocity of geospatial data has created data ecosystems that are highly distributed, heterogeneous, and semantically inconsistent. Existing data catalogs, portals, and infrastructures still rely largely on keyword-based search with limited semantic support, which often fails to capture user intent and leads to weak retrieval performance. To address these challenges, this study proposes a knowledge graph-driven multi-agent framework for intelligent geospatial data discovery, powered by large language models. The framework introduces a unified geospatial metadata ontology as a semantic mediation layer to align heterogeneous metadata standards across platforms and constructs a geospatial metadata knowledge graph to explicitly model datasets and their multidimensional relationships. Building on the structured representation, the framework adopts a multi-agent collaborative architecture to perform intent parsing, knowledge graph retrieval, and answer synthesis, forming an interpretable and closed-loop discovery process from user queries to results. Results from representative use cases and performance evaluation show that the framework substantially improves intent matching accuracy, ranking quality, recall, and discovery transparency compared with traditional systems. This study advances geospatial data discovery toward a more semantic, intent-aware, and intelligent paradigm, providing a practical foundation for next-generation intelligent and autonomous spatial data infrastructures and contributing to the broader vision of Autonomous GIS.

zh.title: 走向智能地理空间数据发现：由大型语言模型支持的知识图驱动多智能体框架
zh.summary: 该框架使用知识图和多智能体协作来改进地理空间数据发现，具有语义理解和意图感知能力，优于传统的基于关键词的系统。它跨越异质元数据标准，使分布式地理空间数据更易发现和语义上更易获取。

fr.title: Vers la Découverte Intelligente de Données Géospatiales : un cadre multi-agents piloté par des graphes de connaissances et alimenté par des grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: Ce cadre utilise des graphes de connaissances et la collaboration multi-agents pour améliorer la découverte de données géospatiales avec une compréhension sémantique et une sensibilisation aux intentions, surpassant les systèmes traditionnels basés sur des mots-clés. Il comble les normes de métadonnées hétérogènes pour rendre les données géospatiales distribuées plus découvrables et sémantiquement accessibles.

de.title: Auf dem Weg zu intelligenter Geordatenentdeckung: Ein wissensgraph-gestütztes Multi-Agent-Framework, das von großen Sprachmodellen unterstützt wird
de.summary: Dieses Framework nutzt Wissensgraphen und Multi-Agent-Zusammenarbeit, um die Geordatenentdeckung durch semantisches Verständnis und Absichtsbewusstsein zu verbessern und übertrifft traditionelle schlüsselwortbasierte Systeme. Es überbrückt heterogene Metadatenstandards, um verteilte Geodaten stärker auffindbar und semantisch zugänglich zu machen.

es.title: Hacia el Descubrimiento Inteligente de Datos Geoespaciales: un marco multi-agente impulsado por gráficos de conocimiento y potenciado por grandes modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: Este marco utiliza gráficos de conocimiento y colaboración multi-agente para mejorar el descubrimiento de datos geoespaciales con comprensión semántica y conciencia de intenciones, superando los sistemas tradicionales basados en palabras clave. Cierra la brecha entre estándares de metadatos heterogéneos para hacer que los datos geoespaciales distribuidos sean más detectables y accesibles semánticamente.

title: AI-Driven Multi-Agent Simulation of Stratified Polyamory Systems: A Computational Framework for Optimizing Social Reproductive Efficiency
summary: This paper applies multi-agent modeling and reinforcement learning to simulate alternative relationship structures addressing declining fertility rates and demographic challenges. Using computational social science methods, the authors model stratified polyamory as a potential mechanism for improving aggregate social welfare.
content:
arXiv:2603.20678v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Contemporary societies face a severe crisis of demographic reproduction. Global fertility rates continue to decline precipitously, with East Asian nations exhibiting the most dramatic trends -- China's total fertility rate (TFR) fell to approximately 1.0 in 2023, while South Korea's dropped below 0.72. Simultaneously, the institution of marriage is undergoing structural disintegration: educated women rationally reject unions lacking both emotional fulfillment and economic security, while a growing proportion of men at the lower end of the socioeconomic spectrum experience chronic sexual deprivation, anxiety, and learned helplessness. This paper proposes a computational framework for modeling and evaluating a Stratified Polyamory System (SPS) using techniques from agent-based modeling (ABM), multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), and large language model (LLM)-empowered social simulation. The SPS permits individuals to maintain a limited number of legally recognized secondary partners in addition to one primary spouse, combined with socialized child-rearing and inheritance reform. We formalize the A/B/C stratification as heterogeneous agent types in a multi-agent system and model the matching process as a MARL problem amenable to Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO). The mating network is analyzed using graph neural network (GNN) representations. Drawing on evolutionary psychology, behavioral ecology, social stratification theory, computational social science, algorithmic fairness, and institutional economics, we argue that SPS can improve aggregate social welfare in the Pareto sense. Preliminary computational results demonstrate the framework's viability in addressing the dual crisis of female motherhood penalties and male sexlessness, while offering a non-violent mechanism for wealth dispersion analogous to the historical Chinese Grace Decree (Tui'en Ling).

zh.title: AI驱动的分层多元关系系统的多智能体模拟：优化社会生殖效率的计算框架
zh.summary: 本文应用多智能体建模和强化学习来模拟替代性关系结构，以解决生育率下降和人口挑战。使用计算社会科学方法，作者们将分层多元关系建模为改进总体社会福利的潜在机制。

fr.title: Simulation Multi-Agents Pilotée par l'IA des Systèmes de Polyamour Stratifiés : Un Cadre Computationnel pour Optimiser l'Efficacité Reproductive Sociale
fr.summary: Cet article applique la modélisation multi-agents et l'apprentissage par renforcement pour simuler des structures relationnelles alternatives abordant les taux de fécondité en déclin et les défis démographiques. Utilisant des méthodes de science sociale computationnelle, les auteurs modèlent la polyamour stratifiée comme un mécanisme potentiel pour améliorer le bien-être social global.

de.title: Von KI angetriebene Multi-Agent-Simulation geschichteter Polyamorie-Systeme: Ein Rechenrahmen zur Optimierung der sozialen Reproduktionseffizienz
de.summary: Dieses Papier wendet Multi-Agent-Modellierung und verstärkendes Lernen an, um alternative Beziehungsstrukturen zu simulieren, die sinkende Geburtenraten und demografische Herausforderungen adressieren. Unter Verwendung von Methoden der computergestützten Sozialwissenschaften modellieren die Autoren geschichtete Polyamorie als einen potenziellen Mechanismus zur Verbesserung der Gesamtsozialwohlfahrt.

es.title: Simulación Multi-Agente Impulsada por IA de Sistemas de Poliamor Estratificados: Un Marco Computacional para Optimizar la Eficiencia Reproductiva Social
es.summary: Este artículo aplica modelado multi-agente y aprendizaje por refuerzo para simular estructuras de relaciones alternativas que aborden las tasas de fertilidad decrecientes y desafíos demográficos. Utilizando métodos de ciencia social computacional, los autores modelan la poliamor estratificada como un mecanismo potencial para mejorar el bienestar social agregado.

title: Multi-RF Fusion with Multi-GNN Blending for Molecular Property Prediction
summary: Multi-RF Fusion achieves top-1 performance on the OGB molecular property prediction benchmark by combining random forests trained on molecular fingerprints with deep-learning GNNs. Key innovations include optimized feature settings and seed averaging that reduce variance while maintaining simplicity without external data.
content:
arXiv:2603.20724v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multi-RF Fusion achieves a test ROC-AUC of 0.8476 +/- 0.0002 on ogbg-molhiv (10 seeds), placing #1 on the OGB leaderboard ahead of HyperFusion (0.8475 +/- 0.0003). The core of the method is a rank-averaged ensemble of 12 Random Forest models trained on concatenated molecular fingerprints (FCFP, ECFP, MACCS, atom pairs -- 4,263 dimensions total), blended with deep-ensembled GNN predictions at 12% weight. Two findings drive the result: (1) setting max_features to 0.20 instead of the default sqrt(d) gives a +0.008 AUC gain on this scaffold split, and (2) averaging GNN predictions across 10 seeds before blending with the RF eliminates GNN seed variance entirely, dropping the final standard deviation from 0.0008 to 0.0002. No external data or pre-training is used.

zh.title: 分子性质预测的多随机森林融合与多GNN混合方法
zh.summary: 多RF融合在OGB分子性质预测基准上取得最佳性能，通过将在分子指纹上训练的随机森林与深度学习GNN相结合。主要创新包括优化的特征设置和种子平均化，可在不使用外部数据的情况下减少方差并保持简洁性。

fr.title: Fusion Multi-RF avec Mélange Multi-GNN pour la Prédiction des Propriétés Moléculaires
fr.summary: La fusion Multi-RF atteint une performance de premier rang sur l'indice de référence OGB pour la prédiction des propriétés moléculaires en combinant des forêts aléatoires entraînées sur des empreintes moléculaires avec des GNN d'apprentissage profond. Les innovations clés incluent des paramètres de fonctionnalités optimisés et une moyenne des graines qui réduisent la variance tout en maintenant la simplicité sans données externes.

de.title: Multi-RF-Fusion mit Multi-GNN-Blending zur Vorhersage molekularer Eigenschaften
de.summary: Multi-RF-Fusion erreicht eine Leistung auf Platz 1 bei der OGB-Benchmark für die Vorhersage molekularer Eigenschaften durch die Kombination von Zufallswäldern, die auf molekularen Fingerabdrücken trainiert sind, mit tiefem Lernen GNNs. Zu den Schlüsselinnovationen gehören optimierte Funktionseinstellungen und Seed-Averaging, die die Varianz verringern und gleichzeitig die Einfachheit ohne externe Daten beibehalten.

es.title: Fusión Multi-RF con Mezcla Multi-GNN para la Predicción de Propiedades Moleculares
es.summary: La fusión Multi-RF logra un rendimiento de primer lugar en el punto de referencia OGB de predicción de propiedades moleculares al combinar bosques aleatorios entrenados en huellas dactilares moleculares con GNN de aprendizaje profundo. Las innovaciones clave incluyen configuraciones de características optimizadas y promediado de semillas que reducen la varianza mientras se mantiene la simplicidad sin datos externos.

title: Modeling Epistemic Uncertainty in Social Perception via Rashomon Set Agents
summary: This framework uses LLM-driven multi-agent modeling to simulate how students develop different subjective views of classroom social structures through limited local information and peer communication. The approach demonstrates how epistemic uncertainty spreads through real-world-like interactions without relying on global information.
content:
arXiv:2603.20750v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present an LLM-driven multi-agent probabilistic modeling framework that demonstrates how differences in students' subjective social perceptions arise and evolve in real-world classroom settings, under constraints from an observed social network and limited questionnaire data. When social information is incomplete and the accuracy of perception differs between students, they can form different views of the same group structure from local cues they can access. Repeated peer communication and belief updates can gradually change these views and, over time, lead to stable group-level differences. To avoid assuming a global "god's-eye view," we assign each student an individualized subjective graph that shows which social ties they can perceive and how far information is reachable from their perspective. All judgments and interactions are restricted to this subjective graph: agents use retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to access only local information and then form evaluations of peers' competence and social standing. We also add structural perturbations related to social-anxiety to represent consistent individual differences in the accuracy of social perception. During peer exchanges, agents share narrative assessments of classmates' academic performance and social position with uncertainty tags, and update beliefs probabilistically using LLM-based trust scores. Using the time series of six real exam scores as an exogenous reference, we run multi-step simulations to examine how epistemic uncertainty spreads through local interactions. Experiments show that, without relying on global information, the framework reproduces several collective dynamics consistent with real-world educational settings. The code is released at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/Rashomonomon-0126.

zh.title: 通过Rashomon集合智能体建模社交感知中的认识论不确定性
zh.summary: 该框架使用大语言模型驱动的多智能体建模来模拟学生如何通过有限的本地信息和同伴交流对课堂社交结构形成不同的主观看法。该方法展示了认识论不确定性如何在不依赖全局信息的情况下通过类似真实世界的交互传播。

fr.title: Modélisation de l'Incertitude Épistémique dans la Perception Sociale via des Agents Ensemble de Rashomon
fr.summary: Ce cadre utilise la modélisation multi-agents basée sur les LLM pour simuler comment les étudiants développent différentes perspectives subjectives des structures sociales de la classe à travers des informations locales limitées et la communication entre pairs. L'approche démontre comment l'incertitude épistémique se propage à travers des interactions semblables au monde réel sans dépendre d'informations globales.

de.title: Modellierung epistemischer Unsicherheit in der sozialen Wahrnehmung über Rashomon-Set-Agenten
de.summary: Dieses Framework nutzt LLM-gesteuerte Multi-Agent-Modellierung, um zu simulieren, wie Schüler durch begrenzte lokale Informationen und Peer-Kommunikation unterschiedliche subjektive Ansichten von Klassensozialstrukturen entwickeln. Der Ansatz zeigt, wie sich epistemische Unsicherheit durch realistische Interaktionen ausbreitet, ohne sich auf globale Informationen zu verlassen.

es.title: Modelado de Incertidumbre Epistémica en la Percepción Social a través de Agentes de Conjunto Rashomon
es.summary: Este marco utiliza modelado multi-agente impulsado por LLM para simular cómo los estudiantes desarrollan diferentes perspectivas subjetivas de las estructuras sociales del aula a través de información local limitada y comunicación entre pares. El enfoque demuestra cómo la incertidumbre epistémica se propaga a través de interacciones similares al mundo real sin depender de información global.

title: GMPilot: An Expert AI Agent For FDA cGMP Compliance
summary: GMPilot is a domain-specific AI agent that helps pharmaceutical companies navigate FDA cGMP compliance by combining a curated regulatory knowledge base with retrieval-augmented generation and reasoning frameworks. It demonstrates practical AI application in highly regulated sectors by improving compliance decision-making responsiveness.
content:
arXiv:2603.20815v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The pharmaceutical industry is facing challenges with quality management such as high costs of compliance, slow responses and disjointed knowledge. This paper presents GMPilot, a domain-specific AI agent that is designed to support FDA cGMP compliance. GMPilot is based on a curated knowledge base of regulations and historical inspection observations and uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Reasoning-Acting (ReAct) frameworks to provide real-time and traceable decision support to the quality professionals. In a simulated inspection scenario, GMPilot shows how it can improve the responsiveness and professionalism of quality professionals by providing structured knowledge retrieval and verifiable regulatory and case-based support. Although GMPilot lacks in the aspect of regulatory scope and model interpretability, it is a viable avenue of improving quality management decision-making in the pharmaceutical sector using intelligent approaches and an example of specialized application of AI in highly regulated sectors.

zh.title: GMPilot：用于FDA cGMP合规的专家AI智能体
zh.summary: GMPilot是一个特定领域的AI智能体，通过将精选的法规知识库与检索增强生成和推理框架相结合，帮助制药公司应对FDA cGMP合规。它通过提高合规决策的响应性，展示了在高度管制行业中AI的实际应用。

fr.title: GMPilot : Un Agent IA Expert pour la Conformité FDA cGMP
fr.summary: GMPilot est un agent IA spécifique au domaine qui aide les sociétés pharmaceutiques à naviguer la conformité FDA cGMP en combinant une base de connaissances réglementaires organisée avec la génération augmentée par récupération et les cadres de raisonnement. Il démontre l'application pratique de l'IA dans les secteurs hautement réglementés en améliorant la réactivité de la prise de décision en matière de conformité.

de.title: GMPilot: Ein Experten-KI-Agent für FDA-cGMP-Konformität
de.summary: GMPilot ist ein domänenspezifischer KI-Agent, der Pharmaunternehmen hilft, die FDA-cGMP-Konformität durch Kombination einer kuratierten behördlichen Wissensdatenbank mit Retrieval-augmented Generation und Reasoning-Frameworks zu bewältigen. Es demonstriert praktische KI-Anwendung in stark regulierten Sektoren durch Verbesserung der Reaktionsfähigkeit bei Compliance-Entscheidungen.

es.title: GMPilot: Un Agente IA Experto para el Cumplimiento FDA cGMP
es.summary: GMPilot es un agente IA específico del dominio que ayuda a las empresas farmacéuticas a navegar el cumplimiento FDA cGMP al combinar una base de conocimientos regulatorios curada con generación aumentada por recuperación y marcos de razonamiento. Demuestra la aplicación práctica de IA en sectores altamente regulados mejorando la capacidad de respuesta de la toma de decisiones de cumplimiento.

title: Governance-Aware Vector Subscriptions for Multi-Agent Knowledge Ecosystems
summary: This work introduces governance-aware subscriptions that match semantic similarity while enforcing regulatory policies across multiple dimensions like data handling and usage rights. The mechanism ensures agents receive only authorized content notifications despite operating under different data policies in shared knowledge bases.
content:
arXiv:2603.20833v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As AI agent ecosystems grow, agents need mechanisms to monitor relevant knowledge in real time. Semantic publish-subscribe systems address this by matching new content against vector subscriptions. However, in multi-agent settings where agents operate under different data handling policies, unrestricted semantic subscriptions create policy violations: agents receive notifications about content they are not authorized to access. We introduce governance-aware vector subscriptions, a mechanism that composes semantic similarity matching with multi-dimensional policy predicates grounded in regulatory frameworks (EU DSM Directive, EU AI Act). The policy predicate operates over multiple independent dimensions (processing level, direct marketing restrictions, training opt-out, jurisdiction, and scientific usage) each with distinct legal bases. Agents subscribe to semantic regions of a curated knowledge base; notifications are dispatched only for validated content that passes both the similarity threshold and all applicable policy constraints. We formalize the mechanism, implement it within AIngram (an operational multi-agent knowledge base), and evaluate it using the PASA benchmark. We validate the mechanism on a synthetic corpus (1,000 chunks, 93 subscriptions, 5 domains): the governed mode correctly enforces all policy constraints while preserving delivery of authorized content. Ablation across five policy dimensions shows that no single dimension suffices for full compliance.

zh.title: 治理感知向量订阅多智能体知识生态系统
zh.summary: 这项工作引入了治理感知订阅，在匹配语义相似性的同时执行多个维度的监管政策，如数据处理和使用权。该机制确保智能体只收到授权的内容通知，尽管在共享知识库中操作时遵循不同的数据政策。

fr.title: Abonnements aux vecteurs conscients de la gouvernance pour les écosystèmes de connaissances multi-agents
fr.summary: Ce travail introduit des abonnements conscients de la gouvernance qui font correspondre la similarité sémantique tout en appliquant les politiques de réglementation sur plusieurs dimensions telles que le traitement des données et les droits d'utilisation. Le mécanisme garantit que les agents reçoivent uniquement les notifications de contenu autorisées malgré leur fonctionnement selon des politiques de données différentes dans les bases de connaissances partagées.

de.title: Governance-bewusste Vektor-Abonnements für Multi-Agent-Wissensökosysteme
de.summary: Diese Arbeit führt Governance-bewusste Abonnements ein, die semantische Ähnlichkeit abgleichen und gleichzeitig regulatorische Richtlinien über mehrere Dimensionen wie Datenverarbeitung und Nutzungsrechte durchsetzen. Der Mechanismus stellt sicher, dass Agenten trotz unterschiedlicher Datenschutzrichtlinien in gemeinsamen Wissensdatenbanken nur autorisierte Inhaltsbenachrichtigungen erhalten.

es.title: Suscripciones a vectores conscientes de la gobernanza para ecosistemas de conocimiento multiagente
es.summary: Este trabajo introduce suscripciones conscientes de la gobernanza que cotejan la similitud semántica mientras aplican políticas regulatorias en múltiples dimensiones como el manejo de datos y derechos de uso. El mecanismo garantiza que los agentes reciban solo notificaciones de contenido autorizadas a pesar de operar bajo diferentes políticas de datos en bases de conocimiento compartidas.

title: ReLaMix: Residual Latency-Aware Mixing for Delay-Robust Financial Time-Series Forecasting
summary: ReLaMix extends existing time-series mixers with learnable compression and residual refinement to handle delayed or stale observations common in high-frequency financial markets. Testing on real market data shows state-of-the-art accuracy across multiple delay conditions with significantly fewer parameters than transformer baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.20869v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Financial time-series forecasting in real-world high-frequency markets is often hindered by delayed or partially stale observations caused by asynchronous data acquisition and transmission latency. To better reflect such practical conditions, we investigate a simulated delay setting where a portion of historical signals is corrupted by a Zero-Order Hold (ZOH) mechanism, significantly increasing forecasting difficulty through stepwise stagnation artifacts. In this paper, we propose ReLaMix (Residual Latency-Aware Mixing Network), a lightweight extension of TimeMixer that integrates learnable bottleneck compression with residual refinement for robust signal recovery under delayed observations. ReLaMix explicitly suppresses redundancy from repeated stale values while preserving informative market dynamics via residual mixing enhancement. Experiments on a large-scale second-resolution PAXGUSDT benchmark demonstrate that ReLaMix consistently achieves state-of-the-art accuracy across multiple delay ratios and prediction horizons, outperforming strong mixer and Transformer baselines with substantially fewer parameters. Moreover, additional evaluations on BTCUSDT confirm the cross-asset generalization ability of the proposed framework. These results highlight the effectiveness of residual bottleneck mixing for high-frequency financial forecasting under realistic latency-induced staleness.

zh.title: ReLaMix：用于延迟稳健金融时间序列预测的残差延迟感知混合
zh.summary: ReLaMix通过可学习的压缩和残差细化扩展了现有的时间序列混合器，以处理高频金融市场中常见的延迟或陈旧观测。在真实市场数据上的测试显示，在多种延迟条件下，与transformer基线相比，参数数量显著减少，准确度处于最先进水平。

fr.title: ReLaMix : Mélange conscient de la latence résiduelle pour la prévision des séries chronologiques financières robustes aux retards
fr.summary: ReLaMix étend les mixeurs de séries chronologiques existants avec une compression apprenable et un raffinement résiduel pour gérer les observations retardées ou obsolètes courantes sur les marchés financiers à haute fréquence. Les tests sur des données de marché réelles montrent une précision de pointe sur plusieurs conditions de retard avec significativement moins de paramètres que les modèles de base de transformateur.

de.title: ReLaMix: Residuelle Latenz-bewusste Mischung für verzögerungsrobuste finanzielle Zeitreihenprognosen
de.summary: ReLaMix erweitert bestehende Zeitreihen-Mixer mit lernbarer Kompression und residueller Verfeinerung, um verzögerte oder veraltete Beobachtungen zu verarbeiten, die auf hochfrequenten Finanzmärkten häufig vorkommen. Tests mit realen Marktdaten zeigen modernste Genauigkeit über mehrere Verzögerungsbedingungen hinweg mit deutlich weniger Parametern als Transformer-Baselines.

es.title: ReLaMix: Mezcla consciente de latencia residual para pronósticos de series de tiempo financieras resistentes a retrasos
es.summary: ReLaMix extiende los mezcladores de series de tiempo existentes con compresión aprendible y refinamiento residual para manejar observaciones retrasadas u obsoletas comunes en mercados financieros de alta frecuencia. Las pruebas con datos de mercado reales muestran una precisión de última generación en múltiples condiciones de retraso con significativamente menos parámetros que los modelos base de transformador.

title: Do LLM-Driven Agents Exhibit Engagement Mechanisms? Controlled Tests of Information Load, Descriptive Norms, and Popularity Cues
summary: Researchers tested whether LLM-driven agents in simulated social media environments respond to engagement mechanisms in theoretically meaningful ways, finding that behavior varies systematically with information load and social norms rather than rigidly following prompts. The study validates agent-based simulation methodology for communication research.
content:
arXiv:2603.20911v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models make agent-based simulation more behaviorally expressive, but they also sharpen a basic methodological tension: fluent, human-like output is not, by itself, evidence for theory. We evaluate what an LLM-driven simulation can credibly support using information engagement on social media as a test case. In a Weibo-like environment, we manipulate information load and descriptive norms, while allowing popularity cues (cumulative likes and Sina Weibo-style cumulative reshares) to evolve endogenously. We then ask whether simulated behavior changes in theoretically interpretable ways under these controlled variations, rather than merely producing plausible-looking traces. Engagement responds systematically to information load and descriptive norms, and sensitivity to popularity cues varies across contexts, indicating conditionality rather than rigid prompt compliance. We discuss methodological implications for simulation-based communication research, including multi-condition stress tests, explicit no-norm baselines because default prompts are not blank controls, and design choices that preserve endogenous feedback loops when studying bandwagon dynamics.

zh.title: 大语言模型驱动的智能体是否表现出参与机制？信息负载、描述性规范和流行度线索的对照测试
zh.summary: 研究人员测试了模拟社交媒体环境中由大语言模型驱动的智能体是否以理论上有意义的方式对参与机制做出反应，发现行为随着信息负载和社会规范而系统地变化，而不是僵化地遵循提示。这项研究验证了用于通信研究的基于智能体的模拟方法。

fr.title: Les agents pilotés par LLM présentent-ils des mécanismes d'engagement ? Tests contrôlés de la charge d'information, des normes descriptives et des signaux de popularité
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont testé si les agents pilotés par LLM dans des environnements de médias sociaux simulés réagissent aux mécanismes d'engagement de manière théoriquement significative, découvrant que le comportement varie systématiquement avec la charge d'information et les normes sociales plutôt que de suivre rigidement les invites. L'étude valide la méthodologie de simulation basée sur les agents pour la recherche en communication.

de.title: Zeigen von LLM gesteuerte Agenten Engagementmechanismen? Kontrollierte Tests von Informationslast, deskriptiven Normen und Popularitätssignalen
de.summary: Forscher testeten, ob von LLM gesteuerte Agenten in simulierten sozialen Medienumgebungen auf Engagementmechanismen theoretisch sinnvoll reagieren, und stellten fest, dass das Verhalten mit der Informationslast und sozialen Normen systematisch variiert, anstatt starrköpfig Aufforderungen zu befolgen. Die Studie validiert die agentenbasierte Simulationsmethodik für die Kommunikationsforschung.

es.title: ¿Exhiben los agentes impulsados por LLM mecanismos de participación? Pruebas controladas de carga de información, normas descriptivas y señales de popularidad
es.summary: Los investigadores probaron si los agentes impulsados por LLM en entornos de redes sociales simulados responden a mecanismos de participación de manera teóricamente significativa, encontrando que el comportamiento varía sistemáticamente con la carga de información y las normas sociales en lugar de seguir rígidamente las indicaciones. El estudio valida la metodología de simulación basada en agentes para la investigación en comunicación.

title: Profit is the Red Team: Stress-Testing Agents in Strategic Economic Interactions
summary: Researchers propose profit-driven red teaming to stress-test agentic systems by training learned opponents to exploit vulnerabilities through adaptive strategies. Testing reveals that agents appearing robust against static baselines become exploitable under profit-optimized pressure.
content:
arXiv:2603.20925v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As agentic systems move into real-world deployments, their decisions increasingly depend on external inputs such as retrieved content, tool outputs, and information provided by other actors. When these inputs can be strategically shaped by adversaries, the relevant security risk extends beyond a fixed library of prompt attacks to adaptive strategies that steer agents toward unfavorable outcomes. We propose profit-driven red teaming, a stress-testing protocol that replaces handcrafted attacks with a learned opponent trained to maximize its profit using only scalar outcome feedback. The protocol requires no LLM-as-judge scoring, attack labels, or attack taxonomy, and is designed for structured settings with auditable outcomes. We instantiate it in a lean arena of four canonical economic interactions, which provide a controlled testbed for adaptive exploitability. In controlled experiments, agents that appear strong against static baselines become consistently exploitable under profit-optimized pressure, and the learned opponent discovers probing, anchoring, and deceptive commitments without explicit instruction. We then distill exploit episodes into concise prompt rules for the agent, which make most previously observed failures ineffective and substantially improve target performance. These results suggest that profit-driven red-team data can provide a practical route to improving robustness in structured agent settings with auditable outcomes.

zh.title: 利润是红队：在战略经济互动中压力测试代理系统
zh.summary: 研究人员提出利润驱动的红队测试方法，通过训练学到的对手来利用漏洞，采用自适应策略对代理系统进行压力测试。测试表明，在静态基线上看起来强大的代理在利润优化压力下变得可被利用。

fr.title: Profit est l'équipe rouge : Tests de charge des agents dans les interactions économiques stratégiques
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent une équipe rouge motivée par le profit pour tester les systèmes d'agents en formant des adversaires appris pour exploiter les vulnérabilités par des stratégies adaptatives. Les tests révèlent que les agents semblant robustes contre les lignes de base statiques deviennent exploitables sous la pression optimisée pour le profit.

de.title: Gewinn ist das Red Team: Stresstests von Agenten in strategischen wirtschaftlichen Interaktionen
de.summary: Forscher schlagen Profit-orientierte Red-Team-Tests vor, um agentenbasierte Systeme zu testen, indem sie erlernte Gegner trainieren, um Schwachstellen durch adaptive Strategien auszunutzen. Tests zeigen, dass Agenten, die gegen statische Baselines robust erscheinen, unter Profit-optimiertem Druck anfällig werden.

es.title: Ganancias es el Equipo Rojo: Pruebas de Estrés de Agentes en Interacciones Económicas Estratégicas
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen pruebas de equipo rojo impulsadas por ganancias para probar sistemas de agentes entrenando a oponentes aprendidos para explotar vulnerabilidades a través de estrategias adaptativas. Las pruebas revelan que los agentes que parecen robustos contra baselines estáticas se vuelven explotables bajo presión optimizada por ganancias.

title: gUFO: A Gentle Foundational Ontology for Semantic Web Knowledge Graphs
summary: Researchers present gUFO, a lightweight implementation of the Unified Foundational Ontology for semantic web applications undergoing ISO standardization. The system provides patterns for knowledge graphs with advantages over existing foundational ontology implementations.
content:
arXiv:2603.20948v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: gUFO is a lightweight implementation of the Unified Foundational Ontology (UFO) suitable for Semantic Web OWL 2 DL applications. UFO is a mature foundational ontology with a rich axiomatization and that has been employed in a significant number of projects in research and industry. Moreover, it is currently in the process of standardization by the International Organization for Standardization as the ISO/IEC CD 21838-5. gUFO stands out from other foundational ontology implementations (such as those provided for BFO and DOLCE) given its unique support for a typology of types (operationalizing OntoClean guidelines), its reification patterns for intrinsic and relational aspects, and its support for situations and high-order types. gUFO provides well-founded patterns to address recurrent problems in Semantic Web knowledge graphs. In this paper, we present gUFO with its constituting categories, relations and constraints, discuss how it differs from the original UFO reference ontology, elaborate on its community adoption, and systematically position it in relation to existing OWL-based implementations of popular alternative foundational ontologies.

zh.title: gUFO：语义网知识图谱的温和基础本体
zh.summary: 研究人员推出gUFO，这是统一基础本体的轻量级实现，用于正在进行ISO标准化的语义网应用。该系统为知识图谱提供模式，相比现有的基础本体实现具有优势。

fr.title: gUFO : Une ontologie fondationnelle douce pour les graphes de connaissances du Web sémantique
fr.summary: Les chercheurs présentent gUFO, une implémentation légère de l'ontologie fondationnelle unifiée pour les applications de web sémantique en cours de normalisation ISO. Le système fournit des modèles pour les graphes de connaissances avec des avantages par rapport aux implémentations d'ontologies fondationnelles existantes.

de.title: gUFO: Eine sanfte Gründungsontologie für semantische Wissensgraphen
de.summary: Forscher präsentieren gUFO, eine leichte Implementierung der einheitlichen Gründungsontologie für semantische Webanwendungen, die sich einer ISO-Standardisierung unterziehen. Das System bietet Muster für Wissensgraphen mit Vorteilen gegenüber bestehenden Gründungsontologie-Implementierungen.

es.title: gUFO: Una Ontología Fundacional Amable para Gráficos de Conocimiento de la Web Semántica
es.summary: Los investigadores presentan gUFO, una implementación ligera de la Ontología Fundacional Unificada para aplicaciones de web semántico bajo estandarización ISO. El sistema proporciona patrones para gráficos de conocimiento con ventajas sobre las implementaciones de ontologías fundacionales existentes.

title: AutoMOOSE: An Agentic AI for Autonomous Phase-Field Simulation
summary: AutoMOOSE is an agentic framework that automates the full simulation lifecycle in materials modeling by orchestrating a five-agent pipeline from natural-language prompts. Validated on copper grain growth simulations, it demonstrates autonomous error diagnosis and substantial computational speedups.
content:
arXiv:2603.20986v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multiphysics simulation frameworks such as MOOSE provide rigorous engines for phase-field materials modeling, yet adoption is constrained by the expertise required to construct valid input files, coordinate parameter sweeps, diagnose failures, and extract quantitative results. We introduce AutoMOOSE, an open-source agentic framework that orchestrates the full simulation lifecycle from a single natural-language prompt. AutoMOOSE deploys a five-agent pipeline in which the Input Writer coordinates six sub-agents and the Reviewer autonomously corrects runtime failures without user intervention. A modular plugin architecture enables new phase-field formulations without modifying the core framework, and a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server exposes the workflow as ten structured tools for interoperability with any MCP-compatible client. Validated on a four-temperature copper grain growth benchmark, AutoMOOSE generates MOOSE input files with 6 of 12 structural blocks matching a human expert reference exactly and 4 functionally equivalent, executes all runs in parallel with a 1.8x speedup, and performs an end-to-end physical consistency check spanning intent, finite-element execution, and Arrhenius kinetics with no human verification. Grain coarsening kinetics are recovered with R^2 = 0.90-0.95 at T >= 600 K; the recovered activation energy Q_fit = 0.296 eV is consistent with a human-written reference (Q_fit = 0.267 eV) under identical parameters. Three runtime failure classes were diagnosed and resolved autonomously within a single correction cycle, and every run produces a provenance record satisfying FAIR data principles. These results show that the gap between knowing the physics and executing a validated simulation campaign can be bridged by a lightweight multi-agent orchestration layer, providing a pathway toward AI-driven materials discovery and self-driving laboratories.

zh.title: AutoMOOSE：用于自主相场模拟的代理AI
zh.summary: AutoMOOSE是一个代理框架，通过编排一个五代理管道来自动化材料建模中的完整模拟生命周期，该管道由自然语言提示驱动。在铜晶粒生长模拟中验证，它展示了自主错误诊断和显著的计算加速。

fr.title: AutoMOOSE : Une IA multi-agents pour la simulation autonome du champ de phase
fr.summary: AutoMOOSE est un cadre multi-agents qui automatise le cycle de vie complet de la simulation en modélisation des matériaux en orchestrant un pipeline de cinq agents à partir d'invites en langage naturel. Validé sur des simulations de croissance de grains de cuivre, il démontre un diagnostic d'erreur autonome et des accélérations informatiques substantielles.

de.title: AutoMOOSE: Ein agentenbasiertes KI-System für autonome Phasenfeldsimulatione
de.summary: AutoMOOSE ist ein agentenbasiertes Framework, das den vollständigen Simulationslebenszyklus in der Materialmodellierung durch Orchestrierung einer fünf-Agenten-Pipeline von natürlichsprachigen Eingaben automatisiert. Validiert bei Kupferkornwachstumssimulationen zeigt es autonome Fehlerdiagnose und erhebliche Rechenbeschleunigungen.

es.title: AutoMOOSE: Una IA Agéntica para Simulación Autónoma de Campo de Fase
es.summary: AutoMOOSE es un framework agéntico que automatiza el ciclo de vida completo de simulación en modelado de materiales orquestando un pipeline de cinco agentes desde indicaciones en lenguaje natural. Validado en simulaciones de crecimiento de grano de cobre, demuestra diagnóstico autónomo de errores y aceleraciones computacionales sustanciales.

title: Can we automatize scientific discovery in the cognitive sciences?
summary: Researchers propose a fully automated framework for cognitive science discovery that uses LLMs to sample experimental paradigms, simulate behavioral data, and synthesize cognitive models at scale. The system functions as a high-throughput discovery engine for exploring computational models of the mind.
content:
arXiv:2603.20988v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The cognitive sciences aim to understand intelligence by formalizing underlying operations as computational models. Traditionally, this follows a cycle of discovery where researchers develop paradigms, collect data, and test predefined model classes. However, this manual pipeline is fundamentally constrained by the slow pace of human intervention and a search space limited by researchers' background and intuition. Here, we propose a paradigm shift toward a fully automated, in silico science of the mind that implements every stage of the discovery cycle using Large Language Models (LLMs). In this framework, experimental paradigms exploring conceptually meaningful task structures are directly sampled from an LLM. High-fidelity behavioral data are then simulated using foundation models of cognition. The tedious step of handcrafting cognitive models is replaced by LLM-based program synthesis, which performs a high-throughput search over a vast landscape of algorithmic hypotheses. Finally, the discovery loop is closed by optimizing for ''interestingness'', a metric of conceptual yield evaluated by an LLM-critic. By enabling a fast and scalable approach to theory development, this automated loop functions as a high-throughput in-silico discovery engine, surfacing informative experiments and mechanisms for subsequent validation in real human populations.

zh.title: 我们能否在认知科学中自动化科学发现？
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了一个完全自动化的认知科学发现框架，使用大语言模型采样实验范式、模拟行为数据并大规模合成认知模型。该系统作为高通量发现引擎，用于探索心理的计算模型。

fr.title: Pouvons-nous automatiser la découverte scientifique en sciences cognitives ?
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent un cadre entièrement automatisé pour la découverte scientifique en sciences cognitives qui utilise les modèles de langage pour échantillonner les paradigmes expérimentaux, simuler les données comportementales et synthétiser les modèles cognitifs à grande échelle. Le système fonctionne comme un moteur de découverte à haut débit pour explorer les modèles informatiques de l'esprit.

de.title: Können wir die wissenschaftliche Entdeckung in der kognitiven Wissenschaft automatisieren?
de.summary: Forscher schlagen ein vollständig automatisiertes Framework für die Entdeckung in der kognitiven Wissenschaft vor, das große Sprachmodelle verwendet, um experimentelle Paradigmen zu sampeln, Verhaltensdaten zu simulieren und kognitive Modelle in großem Maßstab zu synthetisieren. Das System funktioniert als Hochdurchsatz-Entdeckungsmotor zur Erkundung von Rechenmodellen des Geistes.

es.title: ¿Podemos automatizar el descubrimiento científico en las ciencias cognitivas?
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen un marco completamente automatizado para el descubrimiento en ciencias cognitivas que utiliza modelos de lenguaje grande para muestrear paradigmas experimentales, simular datos de comportamiento y sintetizar modelos cognitivos a escala. El sistema funciona como un motor de descubrimiento de alto rendimiento para explorar modelos computacionales de la mente.

title: The Intelligent Disobedience Game: Formulating Disobedience in Stackelberg Games and Markov Decision Processes
summary: Researchers introduce the Intelligent Disobedience Game, a game-theoretic framework formalizing when automated assistants should override human instructions to prevent harm. The framework provides a foundation for training AI systems that can learn safe non-compliance in shared autonomy scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2603.20994v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In shared autonomy, a critical tension arises when an automated assistant must choose between obeying a human's instruction and deliberately overriding it to prevent harm. This safety-critical behavior is known as intelligent disobedience. To formalize this dynamic, this paper introduces the Intelligent Disobedience Game (IDG), a sequential game-theoretic framework based on Stackelberg games that models the interaction between a human leader and an assistive follower operating under asymmetric information. It characterizes optimal strategies for both agents across multi-step scenarios, identifying strategic phenomena such as ``safety traps,'' where the system indefinitely avoids harm but fails to achieve the human's goal. The IDG provides a needed mathematical foundation that enables both the algorithmic development of agents that can learn safe non-compliance and the empirical study of how humans perceive and trust disobedient AI. The paper further translates the IDG into a shared control Multi-Agent Markov Decision Process representation, forming a compact computational testbed for training reinforcement learning agents.

zh.title: 智能不服从游戏：在Stackelberg博弈和马尔可夫决策过程中制定不服从
zh.summary: 研究人员介绍了智能不服从游戏，一个游戏论框架，用于形式化自动化助手何时应该推翻人类指令以防止伤害。该框架为训练能够在共享自主权场景中学习安全不遵从的人工智能系统提供了基础。

fr.title: Le Jeu de Désobéissance Intelligente : Formulation de la Désobéissance dans les Jeux de Stackelberg et les Processus de Décision Markoviens
fr.summary: Les chercheurs présentent le Jeu de Désobéissance Intelligente, un cadre théorique des jeux qui formalise quand les assistants automatisés doivent contourner les instructions humaines pour prévenir les dommages. Le cadre fournit une base pour entraîner les systèmes d'IA qui peuvent apprendre à ne pas se conformer de manière sécuritaire dans les scénarios d'autonomie partagée.

de.title: Das intelligente Ungehorsams-Spiel: Formulierung von Ungehorsam in Stackelberg-Spielen und Markov-Entscheidungsprozessen
de.summary: Forscher stellen das Intelligent Disobedience Game vor, ein spieltheoretisches Framework, das formalisiert, wann automatisierte Assistenten menschliche Anweisungen außer Kraft setzen sollten, um Schäden zu verhindern. Das Framework bietet eine Grundlage für das Training von KI-Systemen, die sichere Nicht-Konformität in gemeinsamen Autonomie-Szenarien erlernen können.

es.title: El Juego de Desobediencia Inteligente: Formulación de la Desobediencia en Juegos de Stackelberg y Procesos de Decisión de Markov
es.summary: Los investigadores presentan el Juego de Desobediencia Inteligente, un marco teórico de juegos que formaliza cuándo los asistentes automatizados deben anular las instrucciones humanas para prevenir daños. El marco proporciona una base para entrenar sistemas de IA que pueden aprender desobediencia segura en escenarios de autonomía compartida.

title: A Framework for Low-Latency, LLM-driven Multimodal Interaction on the Pepper Robot
summary: Researchers present an open-source framework for the Pepper robot integrating end-to-end speech-to-speech models for low-latency interaction and extensive function calling for agentic control. The framework enables LLMs to orchestrate robot actions while preserving paralinguistic cues.
content:
arXiv:2603.21013v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Despite recent advances in integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) into social robotics, two weaknesses persist. First, existing implementations on platforms like Pepper often rely on cascaded Speech-to-Text (STT)->LLM->Text-to-Speech (TTS) pipelines, resulting in high latency and the loss of paralinguistic information. Second, most implementations fail to fully leverage the LLM's capabilities for multimodal perception and agentic control. We present an open-source Android framework for the Pepper robot that addresses these limitations through two key innovations. First, we integrate end-to-end Speech-to-Speech (S2S) models to achieve low-latency interaction while preserving paralinguistic cues and enabling adaptive intonation. Second, we implement extensive Function Calling capabilities that elevate the LLM to an agentic planner, orchestrating robot actions (navigation, gaze control, tablet interaction) and integrating diverse multimodal feedback (vision, touch, system state). The framework runs on the robot's tablet but can also be built to run on regular Android smartphones or tablets, decoupling development from robot hardware. This work provides the HRI community with a practical, extensible platform for exploring advanced LLM-driven embodied interaction.

zh.title: Pepper机器人低延迟、大语言模型驱动的多模态交互框架
zh.summary: 研究人员为Pepper机器人提出了一个开源框架，集成了端到端的语音到语音模型以实现低延迟交互，以及用于代理控制的广泛函数调用。该框架使大语言模型能够编排机器人动作，同时保留语言学线索。

fr.title: Un Cadre pour une Interaction Multimodale Pilotée par Modèles de Langage à Faible Latence sur le Robot Pepper
fr.summary: Les chercheurs présentent un cadre open-source pour le robot Pepper intégrant des modèles de parole à parole de bout en bout pour une interaction à faible latence et un appel de fonction étendu pour le contrôle agentique. Le framework permet aux modèles de langage d'orchestrer les actions du robot tout en préservant les indices paralinguistiques.

de.title: Ein Framework für latenzarme, sprachmodellgesteuerte multimodale Interaktion auf dem Pepper-Roboter
de.summary: Forscher präsentieren ein Open-Source-Framework für den Pepper-Roboter, das End-to-End-Speech-to-Speech-Modelle für latenzarme Interaktion und umfangreiche Funktionsaufrufe für agentische Steuerung integriert. Das Framework ermöglicht es Sprachmodellen, Robotermaßnahmen zu orchestrieren und dabei paralinguistische Hinweise zu bewahren.

es.title: Un Marco para la Interacción Multimodal Impulsada por Modelos de Lenguaje de Baja Latencia en el Robot Pepper
es.summary: Los investigadores presentan un marco de código abierto para el robot Pepper que integra modelos de voz a voz de extremo a extremo para interacción de baja latencia y llamadas de función extensas para control agentivo. El marco permite que los modelos de lenguaje orquesten acciones del robot mientras preservan señales paralingüísticas.

title: Knowledge Boundary Discovery for Large Language Models
summary: Researchers propose Knowledge Boundary Discovery, a reinforcement learning framework that automatically identifies the limits of what LLMs can and cannot reliably answer. The approach mitigates hallucination through iterative exploration and validates findings against manual benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.21022v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We propose Knowledge Boundary Discovery (KBD), a reinforcement learning based framework to explore the knowledge boundaries of the Large Language Models (LLMs). We define the knowledge boundary by automatically generating two types of questions: (i) those the LLM can confidently answer (within-knowledge boundary) and (ii) those it cannot (beyond-knowledge boundary). Iteratively exploring and exploiting the LLM's responses to find its knowledge boundaries is challenging because of the hallucination phenomenon. To find the knowledge boundaries of an LLM, the agent interacts with the LLM under the modeling of exploring a partially observable environment. The agent generates a progressive question as the action, adopts an entropy reduction as the reward, receives the LLM's response as the observation and updates its belief states. We demonstrate that the KBD detects knowledge boundaries of LLMs by automatically finding a set of non-trivial answerable and unanswerable questions. We validate the KBD by comparing its generated knowledge boundaries with manually crafted LLM benchmark datasets. Experiments show that our KBD-generated question set is comparable to the human-generated datasets. Our approach paves a new way to evaluate LLMs.

zh.title: 大语言模型的知识边界发现
zh.summary: 研究人员提出知识边界发现，一个强化学习框架，自动识别大语言模型可以和不能可靠回答的内容的限制。该方法通过迭代探索缓解幻觉，并针对手动基准进行验证。

fr.title: Découverte des limites de connaissances pour les grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent Knowledge Boundary Discovery, un cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement qui identifie automatiquement les limites de ce que les LLM peuvent et ne peuvent pas répondre de manière fiable. L'approche atténue les hallucinations par l'exploration itérative et valide les résultats par rapport aux référentiels manuels.

de.title: Wissensgrenzenerkennung für große Sprachmodelle
de.summary: Forscher schlagen Knowledge Boundary Discovery vor, ein Rahmenwerk für verstärktes Lernen, das automatisch die Grenzen dessen identifiziert, was LLMs zuverlässig beantworten können und nicht können. Der Ansatz reduziert Halluzinationen durch iterative Exploration und validiert Ergebnisse anhand manueller Benchmarks.

es.title: Descubrimiento de límites de conocimiento para grandes modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen Knowledge Boundary Discovery, un marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo que identifica automáticamente los límites de lo que los LLM pueden y no pueden responder de manera confiable. El enfoque mitiga las alucinaciones mediante la exploración iterativa y valida los hallazgos contra puntos de referencia manuales.

title: KLDrive: Fine-Grained 3D Scene Reasoning for Autonomous Driving based on Knowledge Graph
summary: KLDrive combines knowledge graphs with LLM reasoning to enable fine-grained scene understanding for autonomous driving. The framework reduces hallucinations through reliable fact construction and achieves 65% accuracy on NuScenes-QA, significantly outperforming prior methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.21029v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Autonomous driving requires reliable reasoning over fine-grained 3D scene facts. Fine-grained question answering over multi-modal driving observations provides a natural way to evaluate this capability, yet existing perception pipelines and driving-oriented large language model (LLM) methods still suffer from unreliable scene facts, hallucinations, opaque reasoning, and heavy reliance on task-specific training. We present KLDrive, the first knowledge-graph-augmented LLM reasoning framework for fine-grained question answering in autonomous driving. KLDrive addresses this problem through designing two tightly coupled components: an energy-based scene fact construction module that consolidates multi-source evidence into a reliable scene knowledge graph, and an LLM agent that performs fact-grounded reasoning over a constrained action space under explicit structural constraints. By combining structured prompting with few-shot in-context exemplars, the framework adapts to diverse reasoning tasks without heavy task-specific fine-tuning. Experiments on two large-scale autonomous-driving QA benchmarks show that KLDrive outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods, achieving the best overall accuracy of 65.04% on NuScenes-QA and the best SPICE score of 42.45 on GVQA. On counting, the most challenging factual reasoning task, it improves over the strongest baseline by 46.01 percentage points, demonstrating substantially reduced hallucinations and the benefit of coupling reliable scene fact construction with explicit reasoning.

zh.title: KLDrive：基于知识图的自动驾驶细粒度三维场景推理
zh.summary: KLDrive将知识图与大语言模型推理相结合，为自动驾驶提供细粒度的场景理解。该框架通过可靠的事实构建减少幻觉，在NuScenes-QA上达到65%的准确率，明显优于现有方法。

fr.title: KLDrive : Raisonnement fin de scène 3D pour la conduite autonome basé sur le graphe de connaissances
fr.summary: KLDrive combine les graphes de connaissances avec le raisonnement des LLM pour permettre une compréhension fine des scènes pour la conduite autonome. Le cadre réduit les hallucinations par la construction de faits fiables et atteint une précision de 65% sur NuScenes-QA, surpassant considérablement les méthodes antérieures.

de.title: KLDrive: Feinkörniges 3D-Szenen-Reasoning für autonomes Fahren basierend auf Wissensgraph
de.summary: KLDrive kombiniert Wissensgraphen mit LLM-Reasoning, um feines Szenenverständnis für autonomes Fahren zu ermöglichen. Das Framework reduziert Halluzinationen durch zuverlässige Faktenkonstraktion und erreicht 65% Genauigkeit auf NuScenes-QA und übertrifft damit deutlich frühere Methoden.

es.title: KLDrive: Razonamiento fino de escena 3D para conducción autónoma basado en gráfico de conocimiento
es.summary: KLDrive combina gráficos de conocimiento con razonamiento de LLM para permitir la comprensión fina de escenas para conducción autónoma. El marco reduce alucinaciones mediante construcción confiable de hechos y logra una precisión del 65% en NuScenes-QA, superando significativamente a métodos anteriores.

title: LongCat-Flash-Prover: Advancing Native Formal Reasoning via Agentic Tool-Integrated Reinforcement Learning
summary: LongCat-Flash-Prover is a 560-billion-parameter open-source model that advances formal theorem proving in Lean4 through agentic tool-integrated reasoning and hierarchical policy optimization. The model achieves state-of-the-art results on formal reasoning benchmarks with remarkable sample efficiency.
content:
arXiv:2603.21065v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce LongCat-Flash-Prover, a flagship 560-billion-parameter open-source Mixture-of- Experts (MoE) model that advances Native Formal Reasoning in Lean4 through agentic tool-integrated reasoning (TIR). We decompose the native formal reasoning task into three independent formal capabilities, i.e., auto-formalization, sketching, and proving. To facilitate these capabilities, we propose a Hybrid-Experts Iteration Framework to expand high-quality task trajectories, including generating a formal statement based on a given informal problem, producing a whole-proof directly from the statement, or a lemma-style sketch. During agentic RL, we present a Hierarchical Importance Sampling Policy Optimization (HisPO) algorithm, which aims to stabilize the MoE model training on such long-horizon tasks. It employs a gradient masking strategy that accounts for the policy staleness and the inherent train-inference engine discrepancies at both sequence and token levels. Additionally, we also incorporate theorem consistency and legality detection mechanisms to eliminate reward hacking issues. Extensive evaluations show that our LongCat-Flash-Prover sets a new state-of-the-art for open-weights models in both auto-formalization and theorem proving. Demonstrating remarkable sample efficiency, it achieves a 97.1% pass rate on MiniF2F-Test using only 72 inference budget per problem. On more challenging benchmarks, it solves 70.8% of ProverBench and 41.5% of PutnamBench with no more than 220 attempts per problem, significantly outperforming existing open-weights baselines.

zh.title: LongCat-Flash-Prover：通过代理工具集成强化学习推进本地形式推理
zh.summary: LongCat-Flash-Prover是一个5600亿参数的开源模型，通过代理工具集成推理和分层策略优化，推进Lean4中的形式定理证明。该模型在形式推理基准上取得最先进的成果，具有显著的样本效率。

fr.title: LongCat-Flash-Prover : Avancer le raisonnement formel natif via apprentissage par renforcement intégré d'outils d'agent
fr.summary: LongCat-Flash-Prover est un modèle open-source avec 560 milliards de paramètres qui avance la preuve de théorème formel dans Lean4 par le raisonnement intégré à des outils d'agent et l'optimisation de politique hiérarchique. Le modèle atteint des résultats à l'état de l'art sur les points de référence du raisonnement formel avec une efficacité d'échantillon remarquable.

de.title: LongCat-Flash-Prover: Förderung nativen formalen Reasonings durch agentenintegriertes Tool-Reinforcement Learning
de.summary: LongCat-Flash-Prover ist ein Open-Source-Modell mit 560 Milliarden Parametern, das das formale Theorembeweisen in Lean4 durch agentenintegriertes Tool-Reasoning und hierarchische Richtlinienoptimierung voranbringt. Das Modell erzielt modernste Ergebnisse bei formalen Reasoning-Benchmarks mit bemerkenswerter Stichprobeneffizienz.

es.title: LongCat-Flash-Prover: Avance del razonamiento formal nativo mediante aprendizaje por refuerzo integrado con herramientas de agentes
es.summary: LongCat-Flash-Prover es un modelo de código abierto con 560 mil millones de parámetros que avanza en la demostración de teoremas formales en Lean4 a través del razonamiento integrado de herramientas de agentes y optimización jerárquica de políticas. El modelo logra resultados de última generación en puntos de referencia de razonamiento formal con una eficiencia de muestras notable.

title: ORACLE: Optimizing Reasoning Abilities of Large Language Models via Constraint-Led Synthetic Data Elicitation
summary: ORACLE is a structured data generation framework combining LLM-generated reasoning with symbolic supervision to verify intermediate steps in multi-step reasoning. The approach outperforms baselines on logical, factual, and commonsense reasoning by enabling fine-grained step-level validation.
content:
arXiv:2603.21140v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Training large language models (LLMs) with synthetic reasoning data has become a popular approach to enhancing their reasoning capabilities, while a key factor influencing the effectiveness of this paradigm is the quality of the generated multi-step reasoning data. To generate high-quality reasoning data, many recent methods generate synthetic reasoning paths and filter them based on final answer correctness, often overlooking flaws in intermediate reasoning steps. To enhance the verification of intermediate reasoning steps, prior work primarily resorts to code execution or symbolic reasoning engines. However, code-based validation is restricted to code or mathematical tasks, and reasoning engines require a well-structured and complete context. As a result, existing methods fail to function effectively in natural language reasoning tasks that involve ambiguous or incomplete contexts. In these tasks, synthetic data still lack reliable checks for verifying each reasoning step. To address this challenge, we introduce ORACLE, a structured data generation framework inspired by syllogistic reasoning. ORACLE integrates the generative strengths of LLMs with symbolic supervision: the LLM produces step-wise reasoning contexts, while a symbolic reasoning engine verifies the validity of each intermediate step. By employing a unified prompting template to elicit modular reasoning chains, ORACLE enables fine-grained, step-level validation, facilitating the construction of high-quality multi-step reasoning data. Across six logical, factual, and commonsense reasoning benchmarks, our ORACLE consistently outperforms strong baselines on multiple models.

zh.title: ORACLE：通过约束引导的合成数据提取优化大语言模型的推理能力
zh.summary: ORACLE是一个结构化数据生成框架，结合了大语言模型生成的推理和符号监督，以验证多步推理中的中间步骤。该方法通过启用细粒度步骤级验证，在逻辑、事实和常识推理方面超越了基线方法。

fr.title: ORACLE : Optimisation des capacités de raisonnement des grands modèles de langage via l'élicitation de données synthétiques guidées par des contraintes
fr.summary: ORACLE est un cadre de génération de données structurées combinant le raisonnement généré par le modèle de langage avec la supervision symbolique pour vérifier les étapes intermédiaires dans le raisonnement multi-étapes. L'approche surpasse les lignes de base en raisonnement logique, factuel et de bon sens en permettant une validation au niveau des étapes granulaires.

de.title: ORACLE: Optimierung der Denkfähigkeiten großer Sprachmodelle durch einschränkungsgesteuerte synthetische Datenerzeugung
de.summary: ORACLE ist ein strukturiertes Datenerzeugungsframework, das vom Sprachmodell generiertes Denken mit symbolischer Überwachung kombiniert, um Zwischenschritte beim mehrstufigen Denken zu überprüfen. Der Ansatz übertrifft Baselines beim logischen, faktischen und gesunden Menschenverstand, indem er eine detaillierte Validierung auf Schrittebene ermöglicht.

es.title: ORACLE: Optimización de las capacidades de razonamiento de los grandes modelos de lenguaje mediante la elicitación de datos sintéticos guiados por restricciones
es.summary: ORACLE es un marco de generación de datos estructurados que combina el razonamiento generado por el modelo de lenguaje con supervisión simbólica para verificar pasos intermedios en el razonamiento de varios pasos. El enfoque supera a las líneas de base en razonamiento lógico, fáctico y de sentido común al permitir validación granular a nivel de paso.

title: Can LLMs Fool Graph Learning? Exploring Universal Adversarial Attacks on Text-Attributed Graphs
summary: Researchers propose BadGraph, an attack framework leveraging LLM understanding of graph knowledge to craft universal adversarial attacks on text-attributed graph models. The approach demonstrates effective attacks across both GNN and LLM-based reasoners with up to 76% performance degradation.
content:
arXiv:2603.21155v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Text-attributed graphs (TAGs) enhance graph learning by integrating rich textual semantics and topological context for each node. While boosting expressiveness, they also expose new vulnerabilities in graph learning through text-based adversarial surfaces. Recent advances leverage diverse backbones, such as graph neural networks (GNNs) and pre-trained language models (PLMs), to capture both structural and textual information in TAGs. This diversity raises a key question: How can we design universal adversarial attacks that generalize across architectures to assess the security of TAG models? The challenge arises from the stark contrast in how different backbones-GNNs and PLMs-perceive and encode graph patterns, coupled with the fact that many PLMs are only accessible via APIs, limiting attacks to black-box settings. To address this, we propose BadGraph, a novel attack framework that deeply elicits large language models (LLMs) understanding of general graph knowledge to jointly perturb both node topology and textual semantics. Specifically, we design a target influencer retrieval module that leverages graph priors to construct cross-modally aligned attack shortcuts, thereby enabling efficient LLM-based perturbation reasoning. Experiments show that BadGraph achieves universal and effective attacks across GNN- and LLM-based reasoners, with up to a 76.3% performance drop, while theoretical and empirical analyses confirm its stealthy yet interpretable nature.

zh.title: 大语言模型能欺骗图学习吗？探索针对文本属性图的通用对抗攻击
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了BadGraph，这是一个攻击框架，利用大语言模型对图知识的理解来制造针对文本属性图模型的通用对抗攻击。该方法展示了在图神经网络和基于大语言模型的推理器上的有效攻击，性能降低高达76%。

fr.title: Les modèles de langage peuvent-ils tromper l'apprentissage sur les graphes ? Explorer les attaques adversariales universelles sur les graphes avec attributs textuels
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent BadGraph, un cadre d'attaque exploitant la compréhension du modèle de langage des connaissances sur les graphes pour créer des attaques adversariales universelles sur les modèles de graphes avec attributs textuels. L'approche démontre des attaques efficaces sur les réseaux de neurones graphiques et les raisonneurs basés sur les modèles de langage avec une dégradation des performances jusqu'à 76%.

de.title: Können Sprachmodelle Graphenlernmodelle täuschen? Erforschung universeller adversarialer Angriffe auf textattribuierte Graphen
de.summary: Forscher schlagen BadGraph vor, ein Angriffsframework, das das Verständnis von Sprachmodellen für Graphenwissen nutzt, um universelle adversariale Angriffe auf textattributierte Graphenmodelle durchzuführen. Der Ansatz demonstriert wirksame Angriffe auf beide GNN- und LLM-basierte Reasoner mit einer Leistungsbeeinträchtigung von bis zu 76%.

es.title: ¿Pueden los modelos de lenguaje engañar al aprendizaje de gráficos? Explorando ataques adversariales universales en gráficos con atributos de texto
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen BadGraph, un marco de ataque que aprovecha la comprensión del modelo de lenguaje del conocimiento de gráficos para crear ataques adversariales universales en modelos de gráficos con atributos de texto. El enfoque demuestra ataques efectivos en GNN y razonadores basados en modelos de lenguaje con una degradación del rendimiento de hasta el 76%.

title: Revisiting Tree Search for LLMs: Gumbel and Sequential Halving for Budget-Scalable Reasoning
summary: ReSCALE addresses scaling failures in AlphaZero-style tree search for LLMs by replacing Dirichlet noise with Gumbel sampling and Sequential Halving. The approach restores monotonic accuracy improvements with increased search budget, achieving 58.4% on GSM8K reasoning tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.21162v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Neural tree search is a powerful decision-making algorithm widely used in complex domains such as game playing and model-based reinforcement learning. Recent work has applied AlphaZero-style tree search to enhance the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) during inference, but we find that this approach suffers from a scaling failure: on GSM8K and Game24, accuracy drops as the search budget increases. In this paper, we present ReSCALE, an adaptation of Gumbel AlphaZero MCTS that replaces Dirichlet noise and PUCT selection with Gumbel sampling and Sequential Halving, restoring monotonic scaling without changes to the model or its training. ReSCALE reaches 58.4\% on GSM8K and 85.3\% on Game24 at budgets where the baseline degrades. Ablations confirm that Sequential Halving is the primary driver of the improvement.

zh.title: 重新审视大语言模型的树搜索：Gumbel和顺序折半用于预算可扩展的推理
zh.summary: ReSCALE通过将Dirichlet噪声替换为Gumbel采样和顺序折半来解决AlphaZero风格树搜索中的大语言模型缩放失败问题。该方法随着搜索预算的增加恢复了单调精度改进，在GSM8K推理任务上达到了58.4%。

fr.title: Revisiter la recherche par arborescence pour les modèles de langage : Gumbel et l'élagage séquentiel pour le raisonnement évolutif en budget
fr.summary: ReSCALE aborde les défaillances de mise à l'échelle dans la recherche par arborescence de style AlphaZero pour les modèles de langage en remplaçant le bruit de Dirichlet par l'échantillonnage de Gumbel et l'élagage séquentiel. L'approche rétablit les améliorations de précision monotones avec l'augmentation du budget de recherche, atteignant 58,4% sur les tâches de raisonnement GSM8K.

de.title: Baumsuche für Sprachmodelle neu betrachtet: Gumbel und sequentielle Halbierung für budgetierbare Skalierbarkeit des Denkens
de.summary: ReSCALE behebt Skalierungsfehler in AlphaZero-ähnlicher Baumsuche für Sprachmodelle, indem Dirichlet-Rauschen durch Gumbel-Sampling und sequentielle Halbierung ersetzt wird. Der Ansatz stellt monotone Genauigkeitsverbesserungen bei erhöhtem Suchbudget wieder her und erreicht 58,4% bei GSM8K-Denkaufgaben.

es.title: Revisando la búsqueda en árbol para modelos de lenguaje: Gumbel y bifurcación secuencial para razonamiento escalable por presupuesto
es.summary: ReSCALE aborda las fallas de escalado en la búsqueda en árbol de estilo AlphaZero para modelos de lenguaje al reemplazar el ruido de Dirichlet con muestreo de Gumbel y bifurcación secuencial. El enfoque restaura mejoras de precisión monotónicas con presupuesto de búsqueda aumentado, logrando el 58,4% en tareas de razonamiento GSM8K.
